title: acer italum subsp. hispanicum
intro: Acer italum subsp. hispanicum, also known as the Spanish maple, is a subspecies of the Italian maple tree (Acer italum) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins in its bark and leaves. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Acer italum subsp. hispanicum can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall health.
